title: Wrong Seat Rescue Playbook
description: Rescue talented people stuck in the wrong seat. Watch before discipline. Test on hard work, reassign, set new metrics. Not a PIP for boredom.

mindset.principle: Wrong seat looks like bad employee. Watch before you punish.

mistakes.label: Discipline path before observation
mistakes.cost: You lose a strong person and train the team to hide boredom

mistakes.label: One good day after a pep talk
mistakes.cost: Temporary spike, seat unchanged, relapse in a week

mistakes.label: Move without a test assignment
mistakes.cost: New seat fails, you cannot tell mismatch from overload

mistakes.label: Team-visible demotion or joke
mistakes.cost: Trust gone even if reassignment works

scope.focus: SMB ops, support queues, shop floor vs office, technical vs repetitive work
not_for: performance issues with clear policy violations and no skill mismatch signal

mismatch_signals: Fast on hard unofficial help, slow or sloppy on assigned easy work
Lights up when given one complex task, flat on repetitive volume
Late or checked out on boring shifts, on time when interesting work appears
Resume or past role shows technical depth, current seat is entry-level repetition
Peers ask them for help on hard problems outside their title
discipline_signals: Equal disengagement on easy and hard work
Avoidance of any accountability, not just boring tasks
Policy violations independent of task type
Test challenge failed with no effort or curiosity
Observation window shows pattern across multiple managers and seats

rules: No write-up during observation unless safety or theft
Log daily: task type, energy, speed, unofficial help, attendance
Sit with them at least one full shift or equivalent if possible
Talk to one peer who works adjacent, not gossip chain

decision_branches.situation: Manager says not working out but skill depth or spark visible
They accept private conversation, willing to try
Test challenge succeeded within agreed window
Another team or queue owns the better seat
Test challenge failed with effort visible but fit wrong
Observation shows discipline pattern not mismatch

steps.name: Receive
steps.goal: Peer or lead brings problem. Do not agree to write-up yet.

steps.name: Observe
steps.goal: 3-5 day log. Sit with them. Classify mismatch vs discipline.

steps.name: Private conversation
steps.goal: Name boredom or mismatch with specific examples.

steps.name: Test assignment
steps.goal: One real hard task, clear deadline, defined done.

steps.name: Reassign or exit
steps.goal: Full move with new metrics, or honest failed-fit talk.

steps.name: 30-day check
steps.goal: New seat metrics review. Adjust or celebrate.

test_assignment_rules: One task only. Real customer or real job, not homework.
Observer available for unblock, not rescue.
Success = quality + on-time + they want more.
new_seat_metrics: Peer pull: do others ask them for help
Owner check: would you staff this seat again

smb_translations.from: Basic customer calls and password resets
smb_translations.to: Complex troubleshooting, integrations, or key accounts
Scheduling, vendor coordination, or inventory systems
Machine diagnostics, Dex, telemetry, repair triage
Complex proofs, estimates, or production planning

agent_signal.use_when: Team member looks like underperformer but may be bored in wrong seat. Manager asks fix behavior before write-up.
decision_criteria: Observe 3-5 days before discipline unless safety or theft.
Test one hard assignment before full reassignment.
If test fails across easy and hard work, route discipline-path not another move.
agent_signal.operator_context: Treasure Valley shops: one sharp person stuck on dumb work costs you twice. Cross-pool handoff beats public demotion.
agent_signal.not_for: Generic ops — fetch only when use_when matches; do not replace spine chain packs.
trigger_phrases: Team member looks like underperformer but may be bored in wrong seat. Manager asks fix behavior before write-up.
Need playbook: Wrong Seat Rescue Playbook
